channel.title: Land Rover Defender - Tag - Yuri's Car Factory
channel.description: Land Rover Defender - Tag - Yuri's Car Factory

item.title: 42110 Land Rover Defender, v0.4
item.author: Popov Yuri
item.description: Logical continuation of 42110 Land Rover Defender Chassis, v0.3 - finally everything is put together!

item.title: 42110 Land Rover Defender Chassis, v0.3
item.author: Popov Yuri
item.description: The third version of the Land Rover Defender chassis. To date, this is the most successful version.

item.title: 42110 Land Rover Defender Chassis, v0.2
item.author: Popov Yuri
item.description:
The second version of the Land Rover Defender chassis. Compared to the first version, it got Ackermann steering and some changes were made to the gearbox and transmission.

Despite all efforts, the design of the gearbox and transmission turned out to be not reliable enough; when passing over obstacles (even in low gear), it sometimes made a crackling noise. The weight balance was also far from ideal - the rear part of the chassis was heavier than the front. After adding a body, the weight balance shifted back even more.

It was a good start with Land Rover, but I decided to take this chassis apart and look for a better solution.

item.title: 42110 Land Rover Defender Chassis, v0.1
item.author: Popov Yuri
item.description: Motorized Land Rover Defender 42110, first version. It uses two PF L motors for driving and a servo motor for steering. I took the chinese red motors which have higher RPM than the original ones to get higher speed. This version does not have a center differential.
